title: Metro Fiber Dual-Cut Restoration and Enterprise SLA Triage Optimizer
expected_economic_value: $145k-$260k telecom NOC restoration / infrastructure reliability leadership
normalized_title: Metro Fiber Dual-Cut Restoration and Enterprise SLA Triage Optimizer
instruction: A metro carrier has a dual fiber cut during a storm: one duct cut isolates a hospital district, and a second manhole fire degrades the alternate ring. The NOC needs a restoration sequence, customer communications tiering, and SLA-credit exposure before the next incident bridge. Task packet: outage_start=2026-06-17T03:18Z; ring A sites N1-N2-N3-N4-N5-N1, ring B N3-N6-N7-N5; cut C1 between N2-N3 at OTDR 18.7 km from N2 affects 144 fibers, repair requires crew_type=splice, travel=70 min, setup=40 min, splice_rate=12 fibers/hour, test=35 min; cut C2 manhole fire between N7-N5 affects 48 fibers, requires civil crew travel=120 min, safety_clearance=90 min, splice_rate=8 fibers/hour, test=45 min. Available crews: S1 splice available now, S2 splice available in 2 hours, C1 civil available now; portable DWDM shelf can restore 8x100G channels between N3-N5 after 3 hours install. Customer circuits: HOSP-A 4x10G SLA restore<2h credit=$18,000/hour after 2h path N2-N3-N4 protected=no, EXCH-B 2x100G restore<4h credit=$42,000/hour path N3-N5 protected=yes needs 180G, BANK-C 8x10G restore<3h credit=$12,000/hour path N2-N3 protected=no, CLOUD-D 1x400G restore<6h credit=$55,000/hour path N7-N5 protected=yes, GOV-E 3x10G restore<2h credit=$9,000/hour path N2-N3, MEDIA-F 6x10G best_effort credit=0. Available reroute capacity before repairs: N1-N5 spare=70G, N3-N6-N7-N5 spare=120G but unavailable until C2 safe, microwave emergency=20G setup=60 min latency penalty allowed only for HOSP-A/GOV-E/BANK-C. Rules and constraints: restore life-safety and regulated customers before high-margin commercial unless doing so increases total outage duration for life-safety circuits; SLA credits accrue after threshold in whole 15-minute increments; reroute capacity must meet circuit bandwidth or explicitly partial-service with 40% credit reduction only if customer accepts; civil safety clearance cannot overlap with splice work at C2; compute earliest restoration times under your sequence. Required work: deliver fiber_outage_restoration_optimizer.xlsx + noc_incident_commander_brief.md with timeline, crew assignment, capacity allocation, SLA-credit estimate, partial-service decisions, FCC/NOC notification triggers, and a customer-tiered bridge update. This task is self-contained: use the task packet as the factual record, do not add source references or external datasets, and preserve a high-fidelity audit trail for every calculation, classification, and recommendation. State assumptions and unresolved evidence gaps explicitly. When executed locally, place final deliverables under the artifacts/ directory.
allowed_tools: Local text editor or Markdown editor
Local spreadsheet, CSV, JSON, YAML, or scripting tools for deterministic calculations
Local plotting or diagramming tools when useful
disallowed_tools: External fact lookup or live data pulls
Live production systems, cloud accounts, trading systems, clinical systems, or industrial controls
Secret discovery, credential testing, or any action outside the prepared task workspace
input_requirements: Use the task packet as the sole factual input.
Cite row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, and option IDs from the packet wherever they control a conclusion.
Mark missing, contradictory, or under-supported values as needs_review instead of inventing them.
inputs: All factual inputs are embedded in the task packet.
Use row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, option IDs, and deliverable names exactly as provided.
output_requirements: Produce the required deliverable files with the specified filenames when filenames are provided.
Include intermediate calculations or executable logic sufficient for another reviewer to reproduce the result.
Include validation checks, sensitivity notes around controlling thresholds, and a final decision or recommendation table.
constraints: Use the task packet as the factual record.
Do not browse, call live services, import external datasets, or invent missing values.
Treat regulated or safety-critical outputs as review packets, not authorization to act.
quality_bar: Treat the task as realistic professional work with domain-appropriate rigor.
Preserve the packet's domain detail, identifiers, thresholds, and edge cases in the final deliverables.
Make every recommendation traceable to explicit task-packet evidence or mark it as needs_review.
validation_expectations: Check required fields, row counts, totals, thresholds, and classifications before finalizing.
Show enough formulas, script logic, or tables for an independent reviewer to reproduce the result.
Flag any contradiction, missing input, or threshold-sensitive conclusion.
edge_cases: If packet evidence is incomplete, do not infer missing values; label the item needs_review.
If multiple options satisfy hard constraints, rank them using the packet's stated priorities and explain ties.
If a regulated or safety-critical conclusion appears, frame the output as a review packet rather than authorization to act.
privacy_safety_expectations: Do not use live systems, private accounts, credentials, or external datasets.
Do not include raw secrets or operational instructions that would execute real-world changes.
Keep synthetic identifiers from the packet only when needed for verification.
